title: Meeting Room Finder
description: Match meeting requests to the smallest available room that fits

parts.title: Find best room for each request
parts.description: A coworking space has meeting rooms of different capacities. Some rooms already have bookings. A single room may appear more than once in the bookings array — each appearance represents a separate booking for that room. Implement findRooms(rooms, bookings, requests) / find_rooms(rooms, bookings, requests) that takes an array of room objects (each with an id and capacity), an array of existing booking objects (each with a roomId/room_id, start time, and end time), and an array of request objects (each with a start time, end time, and minimum capacity needed). For each request, return the id of the smallest-capacity room that meets the minimum capacity and has no booking that conflicts with the requested time window. Two time windows conflict if one starts before the other ends and ends after the other starts: `a.start < b.end && b.start < a.end`. Adjacent intervals (one ends exactly when the other starts) do not conflict. If no room is available for a request, return null (or None) at that position. Return the results as an array with the same length as the requests array, in the same order, with null at any index where no room is available. Each request is evaluated independently against the original bookings only.
activeTests: returns null when no room has enough capacity
skips booked rooms during requested time
empty bookings assigns first fitting room
output array length matches requests array length with mixed results
room with multiple bookings is skipped when any booking conflicts
request that fully envelops a booking conflicts
request fully contained within a booking conflicts
partial overlap from the left conflicts
partial overlap from the right conflicts
room at exact capacity threshold is selected
single request with no rooms returns null
unsorted rooms still assigns smallest fitting room
does not mutate the input arrays
